game_name: Might and Magic II: Gates to Another World

danger_signals.visual_form: Varied enemy sprites with increasing visual complexity/menace at higher difficulties
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats in current location; battle imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark, enclosed architectural spaces contrasting with overworld
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger zones; presence of bosses and powerful enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual theme shifts to fire, water, air, or earth dominant aesthetics
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards and elemental damage threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctive chamber layouts separate from standard dungeons
danger_signals.meaning: Critical encounter; class-specific quest completion location

safety_signals.visual_form: Populated areas with NPCs and building structures
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for healing, resting, trading, and quest progression
Character storage and party recovery points

safety_signals.visual_form: Open, well-lit terrain with minimal architectural obstruction
safety_signals.meaning: Lower encounter density; moderate threat level

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Quest objectives related to Corak's soul reunification; spaceship finale location

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Manifestations across multiple planes and time periods
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence across Cron, elemental planes, and temporal instances; sun-fling threat as world-state indicator

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Quest bosses specific to each class (Dread Knight for Knights, Frost Dragon for Paladins, etc.)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Class-restricted dungeon areas; class-specific quest locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard town and overworld aesthetics; normal NPC presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs at fixed locations; standard quest availability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Elemental plane intrusions visible; environmental corruption indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased monster spawn rates; hostile encounters more frequent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same geographic locations rendered in different historical/future states
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different NPC configurations and quest states across nine centuries

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Escalating environmental visual distortion; potential sky/lighting changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Urgency escalation; final boss encounter becomes available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Thematic visual shift to fire, water, air, or earth dominance in affected areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Elemental enemy types appear; environmental hazards reflect plane type

environmental_storytelling.example: Seemingly nonsensical colored messages scattered across land that combine into useful hints; mirrors first game's design
Visual complexity and architectural distinctness of dungeons communicate quest importance and boss proximity
Four elemental planes each have distinct visual identity communicating environmental hazards and thematic context
Visual gating through locked doors and specialized environments that signal party composition requirements
Same locations rendered differently across nine time periods communicate historical context and quest-relevant changes
Shift from fantasy dungeon aesthetics to spaceship interior for final boss signals narrative tone shift and technological revelation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Risk of random death from natural causes; requires health spa visits to reverse aging or spell-based reversal (often fails)
Require daily payment scaled to level; controlled like regular characters but with economic constraint
Deterministic location; class-restricted encounter; victory enables class upgrade
Appears across multiple locations and time periods; threat constant across world states
Different NPC sets and quest configurations across nine centuries; location-based but time-variant

notes: Might and Magic II communicates world-state primarily through environmental theming (overworld vs. dungeon contrast, elemental plane visual shifts) and quest architecture (class-specific locations and bosses). Danger signals rely on dungeon descent and visual complexity rather than explicit HUD warnings. Factions are implicit through quest structure rather than explicit visual faction markers. The game's paradigm shift to sci-fi finale (spaceship) represents ultimate world-state revelation. Time travel mechanic creates visual diegetic storytelling through location-state multiplicity. Character aging and intoxication provide non-combat behavioral state visualization unique for the era.
